title: SEO and Discoverability
shortSummary: Search Engine Optimisation is the practice of making your website and content easy to find when people search for things related to your business. This module covers keyword research, on-page SEO, technical SEO, link building, and how to create content that ranks.
whyItMatters: Over 68% of all online experiences start with a search engine. If your business does not appear when people search for what you offer, you are invisible to the largest source of website traffic on the internet. SEO is a long-term investment that compounds over time. Unlike paid ads, the traffic from SEO does not stop when you stop paying.
coreConcepts: How search engines work: crawling, indexing, and ranking
Keyword research: finding what your customers are searching for
Search intent: informational, navigational, transactional, commercial
On-page SEO: title tags, meta descriptions, headers, content optimisation
Technical SEO: site speed, mobile friendliness, structured data, crawlability
Local SEO for businesses with physical locations
Content and SEO: creating pages that rank and convert
SEO tools and how to use them
Measuring SEO performance: rankings, organic traffic, conversions
